summary:
The agent identifies surface-to-surface contact as the most accurate description of how opposing teeth interact during biting based on established dental principles. Despite a lack of supporting details from the original passage, this choice aligns with foundational concepts in occlusion related to functional tooth interactions. 

Final Answer: $\boxed{\text{D}}$

In normal function, opposing teeth make surface-to-surfac e contact when they come together during chewing—this is essential for proper mastication and force distribution across the dentition. This type of contact ensures stability, prevents excessive wear, and supports efficient food breakdown through coordinated movement of upper and lower arches.
